note: ORIGINAL exam-style items authored for the SOA Exam P Speedrun fork. These use standard probability results and are NOT copied from any real SOA exam. They are the committed, no-copyright FALLBACK held-out set. To evaluate against the official public 'Exam P Sample Questions', drop the extracted items into data/soa_sample_p/items.json (gitignored); the loader prefers that file. See docs/ai-results.md and data/soa_sample_p/README.md. Math is written as MathJax LaTeX (\( \) inline, \[ \] block); answers keep their numeric tokens in original order so the practice-test grader (which reads the final number) is unchanged.
license_note: Original content, AGPL-3.0-or-later with the rest of the fork. Held out of all AI training/index/few-shot; the leakage scan (tools/speedrun/leakage_scan.py) runs over these.

items.question: A number is drawn at random from the integers 1 through 20. What is the probability that it is divisible by 3 or by 5?
For two events, \(P(A) = 0.6\), \(P(B) = 0.5\), and \(P(A \cup B) = 0.8\). Find the probability that both occur.
A committee of 4 is chosen at random from 7 men and 5 women. What is the probability it contains exactly 2 men and 2 women?
How many distinct arrangements are there of the letters in the word LEVEL?
Two components operate independently, each working with probability 0.9. If they are connected in series, what is the probability the system works?
Events \(A\) and \(B\) are independent with \(P(A) = 0.4\) and \(P(B) = 0.7\). Find the probability that exactly one of them occurs.
Suppose \(P(A) = 0.5\) and \(P(B \mid A) = 0.3\). Find the probability that \(A\) and \(B\) both occur.
Two cards are drawn without replacement from a standard 52-card deck. What is the probability that both are aces?
Given that \(P(A \cap B) = 0.24\) and \(P(B) = 0.6\), find \(P(A \mid B)\).
On a rainy day the probability of heavy traffic is 0.8. The probability of rain is 0.3. Find the probability that it rains and there is heavy traffic.
A disease affects 1% of a population. A screening test is 95% sensitive and 90% specific. Given a positive test, what is the probability the person has the disease?
Machine A produces 70% of output with a 1% defect rate; machine B produces 30% with a 4% defect rate. An item is found defective. What is the probability it came from machine B?
A continuous random variable has density \(f(x) = cx\) for \(0 < x < 2\) and \(0\) otherwise. Determine the constant \(c\).
A random variable has cdf \(F(x) = 1 - e^{-x/2}\) for \(x > 0\). Find the probability that it falls between 1 and 3.
A random variable has density \(f(x) = 2x\) on \((0, 1)\). Find its expected value.
A discrete variable takes the values 1, 2, and 5 with probabilities 0.2, 0.5, and 0.3. Find its expected value.
A random variable has density \(f(x) = 2x\) on \((0, 1)\). Find its variance.
A random variable has \(E[X] = 3\) and \(E[X^2] = 13\). Find its standard deviation.
A binomial random variable has 10 trials and success probability 0.3. Find the probability of exactly 2 successes.
Calls arrive at a help desk as a Poisson process averaging 4 per hour. Find the probability that exactly 2 calls arrive in a given hour.
A lifetime is exponential with mean 5 years. Find the probability it exceeds 8 years.
Scores are normally distributed with mean 100 and standard deviation 15. Find the probability a score exceeds 130.
A loss is uniformly distributed on (0, 1000). A policy has an ordinary deductible of 200. Find the expected payment per loss.
A loss is exponential with mean 1000. A policy pays the loss up to a maximum of 2000 (no deductible). Find the expected payment per loss.
A joint density is constant equal to \(k\) on the region \(0 < x < y < 1\). Find \(k\).
For the joint density \(f(x, y) = x + y\) on the unit square, find the probability that both \(X\) and \(Y\) are less than \(\tfrac{1}{2}\).
For the joint density \(f(x, y) = 2\) on \(0 < x < y < 1\), find the marginal density of \(X\).
For the joint density \(f(x, y) = 2\) on \(0 < x < y < 1\), find the conditional density of \(Y\) given \(X = x\).
For the joint density \(f(x, y) = x + y\) on the unit square, find \(E[XY]\).
Suppose \(Y\) is uniform on \((0, 1)\) and, given \(Y = y\), \(X\) is uniform on \((0, y)\). Use the tower rule to find \(E[X]\).
Two variables have \(\mathrm{Var}(X) = 4\), \(\mathrm{Var}(Y) = 9\), and \(\mathrm{Cov}(X, Y) = 3\). Find their correlation coefficient.
Given \(\mathrm{Cov}(X, Y) = -2\), find \(\mathrm{Cov}(2X + 1,\ 3Y - 4)\).
Three independent \(\mathrm{Uniform}(0, 1)\) variables are observed. Find the probability that the maximum is less than 0.8.
Four independent exponential variables with mean 1 are observed. Find the probability that the minimum exceeds 0.5.
\(X\) and \(Y\) are independent with \(\mathrm{Var}(X) = 4\) and \(\mathrm{Var}(Y) = 9\). Find \(\mathrm{Var}(2X - Y)\).
\(X\) and \(Y\) each have variance 1 and \(\mathrm{Cov}(X, Y) = 0.5\). Find \(\mathrm{Var}(X + Y)\).
A sample of 64 independent observations has mean 10 and standard deviation 4. Using the central limit theorem, approximate the probability the sample mean exceeds 11.
The sum of 100 independent variables, each with mean 2 and variance 1. Approximate the probability the sum exceeds 220.
